original_hypothesis.title: Admin Can Bypass Rescue Restriction to Drain Bootstrapping Tokens
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The admin can bypass the safety check in the `rescue` function by temporarily disabling a token's supported status, allowing them to drain all user deposits from the BlastOnboarding contract.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: In `BlastOnboarding.sol`, the `rescue` function has a safeguard: `if (supportedTokens[token]) revert ErrNotAllowed();` to prevent the owner from rug-pulling user deposits. However, the owner also has access to `setTokenSupported(address token, bool supported)`, which allows them to toggle the `supportedTokens` mapping. A malicious or compromised admin can simply call `setTokenSupported(token, false)` and then `rescue(token, attacker, balance)`, completely bypassing the intended trust boundary.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setTokenSupported allows toggling support status, bypassing rescue protection

preconditions: Admin key is compromised or admin acts maliciously

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Admin toggles state, rescues funds, toggles state back
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker steals 100% of the TVL in the bootstrapping contract.

economic_questions: Is the admin a multisig or a single EOA?
validation_requirements: Verify the lack of timelock on `setTokenSupported`

preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Two consecutive blocks or same block if batched

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Right before the bootstrapping phase ends (max TVL)

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All users who deposited into BlastOnboarding

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check admin wallet setup (multisig vs EOA)

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
